nodes.name: model.jaffle_shop.customers
nodes.description: Customer overview data mart, offering key details for each unique customer. One row per customer.

columns.name: customer_id
columns.description: The unique key of the orders mart.

columns.name: customer_name
columns.description: Customers' full name.

columns.name: count_lifetime_orders
columns.description: Total number of orders a customer has ever placed.

columns.name: first_ordered_at
columns.description: The timestamp when a customer placed their first order.

columns.name: last_ordered_at
columns.description: The timestamp of a customer's most recent order.

columns.name: lifetime_spend_pretax
columns.description: The sum of all the pre-tax subtotals of every order a customer has placed.

columns.name: lifetime_tax_paid
columns.description: The sum of all the tax portion of every order a customer has placed.

columns.name: lifetime_spend
columns.description: The sum of all the order totals (including tax) that a customer has ever placed.

columns.name: customer_type
columns.description: Options are 'new' or 'returning', indicating if a customer has ordered more than once or has only placed their first order to date.

nodes.compiled_sql:
with

customers as (

    select * from "demo"."public"."stg_customers"

),

orders as (

    select * from "demo"."public"."orders"

),

customer_orders_summary as (

    select
        orders.customer_id,

        count(distinct orders.order_id) as count_lifetime_orders,
        count(distinct orders.order_id) > 1 as is_repeat_buyer,
        min(orders.ordered_at) as first_ordered_at,
        max(orders.ordered_at) as last_ordered_at,
        sum(orders.subtotal) as lifetime_spend_pretax,
        sum(orders.tax_paid) as lifetime_tax_paid,
        sum(orders.order_total) as lifetime_spend

    from orders

    group by 1

),

joined as (

    select
        customers.*,

        customer_orders_summary.count_lifetime_orders,
        customer_orders_summary.first_ordered_at,
        customer_orders_summary.last_ordered_at,
        customer_orders_summary.lifetime_spend_pretax,
        customer_orders_summary.lifetime_tax_paid,
        customer_orders_summary.lifetime_spend,

        case
            when customer_orders_summary.is_repeat_buyer then 'returning'
            else 'new'
        end as customer_type

    from customers

    left join customer_orders_summary
        on customers.customer_id = customer_orders_summary.customer_id

)

select * from joined

nodes.name: model.jaffle_shop.metricflow_time_spine
nodes.compiled_sql:
-- metricflow_time_spine.sql
with

days as (

    --for BQ adapters use "DATE('01/01/2000','mm/dd/yyyy')"
    
    with date_spine as
(

    





with rawdata as (

    

    

    with p as (
        select 0 as generated_number union all select 1
    ), unioned as (

    select

    
    p0.generated_number * power(2, 0)
     + 
    
    p1.generated_number * power(2, 1)
     + 
    
    p2.generated_number * power(2, 2)
     + 
    
    p3.generated_number * power(2, 3)
     + 
    
    p4.generated_number * power(2, 4)
     + 
    
    p5.generated_number * power(2, 5)
     + 
    
    p6.generated_number * power(2, 6)
     + 
    
    p7.generated_number * power(2, 7)
     + 
    
    p8.generated_number * power(2, 8)
     + 
    
    p9.generated_number * power(2, 9)
     + 
    
    p10.generated_number * power(2, 10)
     + 
    
    p11.generated_number * power(2, 11)
    
    
    + 1
    as generated_number

    from

    
    p as p0
     cross join 
    
    p as p1
     cross join 
    
    p as p2
     cross join 
    
    p as p3
     cross join 
    
    p as p4
     cross join 
    
    p as p5
     cross join 
    
    p as p6
     cross join 
    
    p as p7
     cross join 
    
    p as p8
     cross join 
    
    p as p9
     cross join 
    
    p as p10
     cross join 
    
    p as p11
    
    

    )

    select *
    from unioned
    where generated_number <= 3651
    order by generated_number



),

all_periods as (

    select (
        

    

    cast(cast(
    cast(now() as timestamp)
        at time zone 'UTC' at time zone 'America/Los_Angeles' as timestamp
) as date) + ((interval '1 day') * (-3650))

 + ((interval '1 day') * ((row_number() over (order by 1) - 1)))


    ) as date_day
    from rawdata

),

filtered as (

    select *
    from all_periods
    where date_day <= cast(

    cast(cast(
    cast(now() as timestamp)
        at time zone 'UTC' at time zone 'America/Los_Angeles' as timestamp
) as date) + ((interval '1 day') * (1))

 as date)

)

select * from filtered



)
select
    cast(d.date_day as timestamp) as date_day
from
    date_spine d



),

cast_to_date as (

    select cast(date_day as date) as date_day

    from days

)

select * from cast_to_date

nodes.name: model.jaffle_shop.order_items
nodes.compiled_sql:
with

order_items as (

    select * from "demo"."public"."stg_order_items"

),


orders as (

    select * from "demo"."public"."stg_orders"

),

products as (

    select * from "demo"."public"."stg_products"

),

supplies as (

    select * from "demo"."public"."stg_supplies"

),

order_supplies_summary as (

    select
        product_id,

        sum(supply_cost) as supply_cost

    from supplies

    group by 1

),

joined as (

    select
        order_items.*,

        orders.ordered_at,

        products.product_name,
        products.product_price,
        products.is_food_item,
        products.is_drink_item,

        order_supplies_summary.supply_cost

    from order_items

    left join orders on order_items.order_id = orders.order_id

    left join products on order_items.product_id = products.product_id

    left join order_supplies_summary
        on order_items.product_id = order_supplies_summary.product_id

)

select * from joined

nodes.name: model.jaffle_shop.orders
nodes.description: Order overview data mart, offering key details for each order inlcluding if it's a customer's first order and a food vs. drink item breakdown. One row per order.

columns.name: order_id
columns.description: The unique key of the orders mart.

columns.name: customer_id
columns.description: The foreign key relating to the customer who placed the order.

columns.name: order_total
columns.description: The total amount of the order in USD including tax.

columns.name: ordered_at
columns.description: The timestamp the order was placed at.

columns.name: order_cost
columns.description: The sum of supply expenses to fulfill the order.

columns.name: is_food_order
columns.description: A boolean indicating if this order included any food items.

columns.name: is_drink_order
columns.description: A boolean indicating if this order included any drink items.

nodes.compiled_sql:
with

orders as (

    select * from "demo"."public"."stg_orders"

),

order_items as (

    select * from "demo"."public"."order_items"

),

order_items_summary as (

    select
        order_id,

        sum(supply_cost) as order_cost,
        sum(product_price) as order_items_subtotal,
        count(order_item_id) as count_order_items,
        sum(
            case
                when is_food_item then 1
                else 0
            end
        ) as count_food_items,
        sum(
            case
                when is_drink_item then 1
                else 0
            end
        ) as count_drink_items

    from order_items

    group by 1

),

compute_booleans as (

    select
        orders.*,

        order_items_summary.order_cost,
        order_items_summary.order_items_subtotal,
        order_items_summary.count_food_items,
        order_items_summary.count_drink_items,
        order_items_summary.count_order_items,
        order_items_summary.count_food_items > 0 as is_food_order,
        order_items_summary.count_drink_items > 0 as is_drink_order

    from orders

    left join
        order_items_summary
        on orders.order_id = order_items_summary.order_id

),

customer_order_count as (

    select
        *,

        row_number() over (
            partition by customer_id
            order by ordered_at asc
        ) as customer_order_number

    from compute_booleans

)

select * from customer_order_count

nodes.name: model.jaffle_shop.stg_customers
nodes.description: Customer data with basic cleaning and transformation applied, one row per customer.

columns.name: customer_id
columns.description: The unique key for each customer.

nodes.compiled_sql:
with

source as (

    select * from "demo"."raw"."raw_customers"

),

renamed as (

    select

        ----------  ids
        id as customer_id,

        ---------- text
        name as customer_name

    from source

)

select * from renamed

nodes.name: model.jaffle_shop.stg_locations
nodes.description: List of open locations with basic cleaning and transformation applied, one row per location.

columns.name: location_id
columns.description: The unique key for each location.

nodes.compiled_sql:
with

source as (

    select * from "demo"."raw"."raw_stores"

),

renamed as (

    select

        ----------  ids
        id as location_id,

        ---------- text
        name as location_name,

        ---------- numerics
        tax_rate,

        ---------- timestamps
        date_trunc('day', opened_at) as opened_date

    from source

)

select * from renamed

columns.description: The unique key for each order item.

columns.name: order_id
columns.description: The corresponding order each order item belongs to

with

source as (

),

renamed as (

    select

        order_id,

    from source

)

select * from renamed

nodes.name: model.jaffle_shop.stg_orders
nodes.description: Order data with basic cleaning and transformation applied, one row per order.

columns.name: order_id
columns.description: The unique key for each order.

nodes.compiled_sql:
with

source as (

    select * from "demo"."raw"."raw_orders"

),

renamed as (

    select

        ----------  ids
        id as order_id,
        store_id as location_id,
        customer as customer_id,

        ---------- numerics
        subtotal as subtotal_cents,
        tax_paid as tax_paid_cents,
        order_total as order_total_cents,
        (subtotal::numeric(16, 2) / 100) as subtotal,
        (tax_paid::numeric(16, 2) / 100) as tax_paid,
        (order_total::numeric(16, 2) / 100) as order_total,

        ---------- timestamps
        date_trunc('day', ordered_at) as ordered_at

    from source

)

select * from renamed

nodes.name: model.jaffle_shop.stg_products
nodes.description: Product (food and drink items that can be ordered) data with basic cleaning and transformation applied, one row per product.

columns.name: product_id
columns.description: The unique key for each product.

nodes.compiled_sql:
with

source as (

    select * from "demo"."raw"."raw_products"

),

renamed as (

    select

        ----------  ids
        sku as product_id,

        ---------- text
        name as product_name,
        type as product_type,
        description as product_description,


        ---------- numerics
        (price::numeric(16, 2) / 100) as product_price,

        ---------- booleans
        coalesce(type = 'jaffle', false) as is_food_item,

        coalesce(type = 'beverage', false) as is_drink_item

    from source

)

select * from renamed

nodes.name: model.jaffle_shop.stg_supplies
nodes.description:
List of our supply expenses data with basic cleaning and transformation applied.
One row per supply cost, not per supply. As supply costs fluctuate they receive a new row with a new UUID. Thus there can be multiple rows per supply_id.

columns.name: supply_uuid
columns.description: The unique key of our supplies per cost.

nodes.compiled_sql:
with

source as (

    select * from "demo"."raw"."raw_supplies"

),

renamed as (

    select

        ----------  ids
        md5(cast(coalesce(cast(id as TEXT), '_dbt_utils_surrogate_key_null_') || '-' || coalesce(cast(sku as TEXT), '_dbt_utils_surrogate_key_null_') as TEXT)) as supply_uuid,
        id as supply_id,
        sku as product_id,

        ---------- text
        name as supply_name,

        ---------- numerics
        (cost::numeric(16, 2) / 100) as supply_cost,

        ---------- booleans
        perishable as is_perishable_supply

    from source

)

select * from renamed

nodes.name: model.jaffle_shop.supplies
nodes.compiled_sql:
with

supplies as (

    select * from "demo"."public"."stg_supplies"

)

select * from supplies
